scenario_content:
[Issue Title]: Failure to Identify Contacts

[Post 1]:
Phone: Motorola Triumph
Android: 2.2.2
Provider: Virgin Mobile
This is not a serious issue but it has been bugging me for a while. TextSecure will fail to identify some of my contacts either by not displaying a picture, their name, or both.
I can not figure out why it identifies some contacts with name and picture but not others. I thought it might be because some contacts are synced from my e-mail account and some are stored locally on the phone but I tested this and the account type did not seem to matter.
However, contacts that do not show picture or name on the list view landing page will sometimes have the picture in the thread conversation view, but no name.

[Post 2]:
I can't imagine that this is still a problem after nearly a year. This has been reworked several times. Can you give us feedback if it's still an issue or if it can be closed?

[Post 3]:
Also android 2.2 is not supported, going to close this and @JAV4NkfV can reopen if necessary :).

[Post 4]:
There was no new version for me to install on Froyo so I can't re-install
or update to see if it has been fixed. As it stands on my phone now, the
original problem of non-identification of contacts persists.
I mainly use a Nexus 7 2013 tablet for communication now, and was
disappointed that I can't get TextSecure through the Play store (is it
available for side-load somehow by chance?). The new description on the
app's Play page sounds as if TS doesn't just do sms over cellular anymore
but now uses data connections to send messages like Whatsapp does. If this
is the case then why is there no tablet version?
Hope you guys get a multi-platform version out soon, otherwise Wickr is
going to eat your lunch, when most people will still prefer an open source
option.
Thanks for checking back in with me. :)
On Wed, Apr 16, 2014 at 1:03 PM, Jake McGinty [Masked] <
FWD_1RTbRp2m@opayq.com> wrote:

Abine http://www.getabine.com/ is shielding this email address. This
mail was sent by noreply@github.com.
Click on the link to block this disposible email address: Block this

Learn more about Abine http://www.abine.com/...
Also android 2.2 is not supported, going to close this and @JAV4NkfVhttps://github.com/JAV4NkfVcan reopen if necessary :).

Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHubhttps://github.com//issues/170#issuecomment-40624579
.

[Post 5]:
You can sideload via http://apps.evozi.com/apk-downloader/ but it appears that on your specific tablet TS currently crashes for yet to be determined reasons:
#614
TextSecure does the WhatsApp-style push messages as well as SMS now.
There is no tablet version, because the UI still hast to be adapted for tablets (as mcginty pointed out, they are looking for help on this).
There are also iOS + browser addon versions in development:
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure-iOS
https://github.com/TheBlueMatt/textsecure-chrome/
system_prompt:
你是一位经验丰富的软件工程师和项目管理者，专注于软件系统中的价值风险分析。
你的任务是分析给定的GitHub Issue讨论内容，识别其中涉及的人类价值关切。

价值风险是指软件系统可能对用户或利益相关者的重要价值（如隐私、安全、公平性、可用性等）产生负面影响的潜在问题。
Issue讨论中可能包含用户对软件行为的不满、功能请求、Bug报告等，这些内容可能隐含对某些价值的关切。
user_prompt:
## 价值场景（Issue讨论）
[Issue Title]: Failure to Identify Contacts

[Post 1]:
Phone: Motorola Triumph
Android: 2.2.2
Provider: Virgin Mobile
This is not a serious issue but it has been bugging me for a while. TextSecure will fail to identify some of my contacts either by not displaying a picture, their name, or both.
I can not figure out why it identifies some contacts with name and picture but not others. I thought it might be because some contacts are synced from my e-mail account and some are stored locally on the phone but I tested this and the account type did not seem to matter.
However, contacts that do not show picture or name on the list view landing page will sometimes have the picture in the thread conversation view, but no name.

[Post 2]:
I can't imagine that this is still a problem after nearly a year. This has been reworked several times. Can you give us feedback if it's still an issue or if it can be closed?

[Post 3]:
Also android 2.2 is not supported, going to close this and @JAV4NkfV can reopen if necessary :).

[Post 4]:
There was no new version for me to install on Froyo so I can't re-install
or update to see if it has been fixed. As it stands on my phone now, the
original problem of non-identification of contacts persists.
I mainly use a Nexus 7 2013 tablet for communication now, and was
disappointed that I can't get TextSecure through the Play store (is it
available for side-load somehow by chance?). The new description on the
app's Play page sounds as if TS doesn't just do sms over cellular anymore
but now uses data connections to send messages like Whatsapp does. If this
is the case then why is there no tablet version?
Hope you guys get a multi-platform version out soon, otherwise Wickr is
going to eat your lunch, when most people will still prefer an open source
option.
Thanks for checking back in with me. :)

[Post 5]:
You can sideload via http://apps.evozi.com/apk-downloader/ but it appears that on your specific tablet TS currently crashes for yet to be determined reasons:
#614
TextSecure does the WhatsApp-style push messages as well as SMS now.
There is no tablet version, because the UI still hast to be adapted for tablets (as mcginty pointed out, they are looking for help on this).
There are also iOS + browser addon versions in development:
https://github.com/WhisperSystems/TextSecure-iOS
https://github.com/TheBlueMatt/textsecure-chrome/



## 价值模型参考
以下是可能涉及的价值类型及其定义：
### 人类价值主题 (L2)
- **HV1 (Conformity)**: Adhering to rules, regulations, guidelines, or commonly accepted standards during application use or development.
- **HV2 (Pleasure)**: Providing enjoyment and satisfaction to users when using the application.
- **HV3 (Dignity)**: Maintaining users’ sense of dignity, respect, and personal worth when interacting with the application.
- **HV4 (Inclusiveness)**: Enabling people from different backgrounds, languages, cultures, or knowledge levels to use the application.
- **HV5 (Sense_of_Belonging)**: Supporting users’ feeling of being connected to a group or community through the application.
- **HV6 (Freedom)**: Providing users with options and choices, and avoiding forced dependencies on specific services or workflows.
- **HV7 (Independence)**: Reducing dependency on third-party services or external systems.
- **HV8 (Wealth)**: Considering the monetary or economic cost associated with using the application.
- **HV9 (Privacy)**: Protecting users’ personal information and addressing concerns about data collection or tracking.
- **HV10 (Security)**: Protecting users from external threats or attacks when using the application.

### 系统价值主题 (L3)
- **SV1 (Trust)**: Compelling users to believe that an application is harmless.
- **SV2 (Correctness)**: Ensuring that the application provides expected information and behavior.
- **SV3 (Compatibility)**: Supporting a range of user devices and operating systems.
- **SV4 (Portability)**: Allowing the application to be migrated to other devices.
- **SV5 (Reliability)**: Reducing the occurrence of application failures.
- **SV6 (Efficiency)**: Reducing the time and effort required to perform tasks within the application.
- **SV7 (Energy_Preservation)**: Reducing power or energy consumption during application use.
- **SV8 (Usability)**: Improving the ease of use of the application.
- **SV9 (Accessibility)**: Improving access to information or functionality, particularly for users with special needs.
- **SV10 (Longevity)**: Ensuring prolonged availability of the application in the future.

## 任务
请你以一个软件工程师和项目管理者的视角，回答以下两个问题：

（1）上述Issue讨论是否涉及价值风险？请回答"是"或"否"。

（2）如果涉及价值风险，可能涉及哪些价值风险？请从上述价值模型中选择相关的价值类型，并说明原因。

## 输出格式
请严格按照以下JSON格式输出：
```json
{
  "has_value_risk": true/false,
  "identified_values": [
    {
      "value_id": "价值ID（如HV1, SV1等）",
      "value_name": "价值名称",
      "confidence": 0.0-1.0之间的置信度,
      "reasoning": "识别该价值风险的理由"
    }
  ],
  "overall_analysis": "整体分析说明"
}
```
